findings.description: Digital signature metadata rendered as visible text in the HTML, inserting content between the 'From' and 'Subject' fields that is not visually present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows From: Elizabeth S. Werner / Division of Waste Management / Raleigh Central Office, then directly Subject: ... The HTML inserts three additional lines between them: 'Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner', 'DN: cn=Elizabeth S Werner, o=DWM-Soild Waste, ou=NCDENR, email=elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov, c=US', and 'Date: 2016.04.28 09:56:21 -04:00'. This metadata was embedded in the PDF but not visually rendered; the HTML conversion exposed it as visible text, disrupting reading order.

findings.description: The handwritten cursive signature image of Elizabeth S. Werner visible in the PDF's 'From:' section does not have a corresponding dedicated tag in the HTML. The digital signature metadata text is preserved, but the visual signature mark itself is absent.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a cursive handwritten signature ('Elizabeth S Werner') in the From: area alongside the digital signature metadata. The HTML includes two images: the NC DEQ logo and a 654.4pt-wide image with empty alt text (which by its width and placement does not correspond to the signature). No image specifically represents the handwritten signature.
